name: British History
description: Magna Carta to the Glorious Revolution to Victoria — a thousand years of kings, wars, and turning points.

questions.q: In what year was the Battle of Hastings, in which William the Conqueror defeated King Harold?

source.notes: October 14, 1066 — same year Harold had won at Stamford Bridge against the Vikings.

questions.q: Which 1066 battle saw King Harold defeat a Viking invasion just weeks before Hastings?

source.notes: September 25, 1066. Harold marched south to Hastings immediately afterward.

questions.q: In what year was the Magna Carta sealed by King John at Runnymede?
Approximately how many years did the Hundred Years' War last?
In which French town was the final battle of the Hundred Years' War fought in 1453?

source.notes: Decisive French victory ending English claims to most of France.

questions.q: Who was the mother of Queen Elizabeth I?

source.notes: Henry VIII's second wife. Executed in 1536 when Elizabeth was 2.

questions.q: What was the name of Sir Francis Drake's ship that circumnavigated the globe (1577–1580)?
Which queen reigned over England during the 1707 Acts of Union creating Great Britain?

source.notes: Queen of England/Scotland from 1702; the union of crowns became a single Kingdom of Great Britain in 1707.

questions.q: In 1688, who was invited to take the throne in the so-called 'Glorious Revolution'?

source.notes: William invaded with the support of Protestant nobility; James II fled.

questions.q: How old was Queen Victoria when she came to the throne in 1837?
How long did Queen Victoria's reign last?

source.notes: Surpassed only by her great-great-granddaughter Elizabeth II.

questions.q: Which British king came to the throne in 1936 only because his elder brother abdicated?

source.notes: Edward VIII abdicated to marry Wallis Simpson; portrayed in 'The King's Speech'.

questions.q: Who was Lord Protector of the Commonwealth from 1653 until his death in 1658?

source.notes: Following the execution of Charles I in 1649.

questions.q: Which Welsh nobleman led a rebellion against English rule in the early 15th century?

source.notes: Last native Welshman to hold the title 'Prince of Wales'. Disappeared around 1415.

questions.q: Charles II married which Portuguese princess in 1662, bringing Bombay and Tangier into English hands as her dowry?

source.notes: She is also credited with popularizing tea in England.

questions.q: What was the last successful invasion of mainland Britain (a small French expedition in 1797)?

source.notes: A 1,400-strong French force surrendered after 2 days near Fishguard, Pembrokeshire.

questions.q: Who was the highest-ranking churchman murdered in Canterbury Cathedral in 1170?

source.notes: Killed by knights loyal to Henry II after a long political feud.

questions.q: In what year did Prince Charles get invested as Prince of Wales at Caernarfon Castle?

source.notes: Lavish televised ceremony designed by Lord Snowdon.

questions.q: Who became Prime Minister in 1916 and led Britain through the rest of World War I?
What were the Jacobite Risings of 1715 and 1745 trying to do?
choices: Restore the Stuart dynasty to the throne

source.notes: 1745 ended at Culloden in 1746; Bonnie Prince Charlie fled.
